violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the front retail entrance area and the back door near the bathrooms, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Per the PIC, a workorder is in place for necessary repairs/adjustments. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2024

The walls in the back storage area near the back entrance is in poor repair (large gaps between floor to wall juncture). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Per the PIC, a workorder is in place for necessary repairs. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The door to the outside, located in the front retail entrance area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2024

A trapped mouse was observed under shelving in the retail area. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2024

The walls in the back storage area near the back entrance is in poor repair (large gaps between floor to wall juncture). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

Several pre-packaged food items (see disposal form) were observed adulterated due to one or more of the following reasons, rodent gnawing, direct contact with rodent droppings and/or urine, direct contact from live rodent. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

Inspector observed one dead trapped mouse under shelving in retail aisle as well as one live trapped rodent on shelving in retail aisle. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by live and dead mice observed in retail area, an accumulation of rodent droppings observed in retail and storage areas, and adulterated food products in retail area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelves throughout the retail area have an accumulation of rodent droppings and/or spilled food debris and residue. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
